prerequisites: A running Elasticsearch instance (Elasticsearch 7.x or higher) reachable from Airbyte.
The instance URL to use as the required `endpoint` config field (for example https://your-host:9243).
If authentication is enabled, an Elasticsearch user or API key whose role grants permission to create an index when it does not already exist and to create (index) documents.
For a TLS endpoint with a private or self-signed CA, the CA certificate to supply via the optional `ca_certificate` field.
Optional: SSH bastion host details (host, port, username, and SSH key or password) if reaching Elasticsearch through an SSH tunnel via `tunnel_method`.

authMethods.name: None (no authentication)
authMethods.summary: Connect directly to the `endpoint` without credentials. Set the `authenticationMethod` to none. Suitable only for Elasticsearch instances that do not enforce security.

authMethods.name: API key (apiKeySecret)
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with an Elasticsearch API key. Create one in Kibana (Help menu > Connection details > API key, or the API keys management page) or via the Create API key REST API, which requires the `manage_own_api_key` or `manage_api_key` cluster privilege. The API returns an `id` and an `api_key` value; provide the API key ID and the secret in the `authenticationMethod.apiKeySecret` field.

authMethods.name: Basic auth (username and password)
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with an Elasticsearch username and password. Provide the username and the secret password in the `authenticationMethod.password` field. The account must have permission to create indices and index documents.

setupSteps.title: Set the server endpoint
setupSteps.body: In the destination configuration, enter your Elasticsearch instance URL in the required `endpoint` field, for example https://your-host:9243. This is the only required field.

setupSteps.title: Choose an authentication method
setupSteps.body: Configure `authenticationMethod`. Select None for an unsecured instance, API key to supply an API key ID and secret (`authenticationMethod.apiKeySecret`), or Basic auth to supply a username and password (`authenticationMethod.password`). Ensure the credentials can create indices and index documents.

setupSteps.title: Create an Elasticsearch API key (if using API key auth)
setupSteps.body: In Kibana, open Help > Connection details > API key, or go to the API keys management page and select Create API key; alternatively call the Create API key REST API (requires the `manage_own_api_key` or `manage_api_key` cluster privilege). Copy the returned `id` and `api_key`, then enter them as the API key ID and secret.

setupSteps.title: Configure TLS trust if needed
setupSteps.body: If your Elasticsearch endpoint uses a private or self-signed certificate, paste the CA certificate into the optional `ca_certificate` field so the TLS connection can be verified.

setupSteps.title: Set optional endpoint and write options
setupSteps.body: Optionally set `pathPrefix` if your Elasticsearch is served under a custom path prefix, and enable `upsert` to update existing records instead of only appending. Both are optional and default to the connector defaults.

setupSteps.title: Configure an SSH tunnel if required
setupSteps.body: If Elasticsearch is only reachable through a bastion, set `tunnel_method` with the jump server host, port, and SSH login user, and provide either an SSH private key (`tunnel_method.ssh_key`) or a tunnel user password (`tunnel_method.tunnel_user_password`).

sources.title: Elasticsearch API documentation - Create an API key
